question_text: Explain why the UK receives more solar energy in summer than in winter, even though it's the same distance from the Sun.
correct_answer: In summer, the Sun's rays hit the UK at a steeper angle (more directly), concentrating the energy over a smaller area. In winter, the rays hit at a shallow angle, spreading the same energy over a larger area. Additionally, days are longer in summer.
explanation: When the Sun is high in the sky, its rays are nearly vertical and concentrated. When the Sun is low in the sky, the same rays spread out over a wider area. This is why a steeper angle gives more heating effect. Combined with longer daylight hours in summer, this explains why summer is warmer.
